gates: root and template Project Control validate, bootstrap, and doctor pass
proportional Outcome-First and Reuse-First full preflight passes
short mode is limited to bounded local work without conditional scope
features, integrations, external actions, and product experiments require full mode
schema v1 passes only when its exact file SHA-256 is registered in current hash-verified Project Control acceptance
new contracts use schema v2 with planned-path and behavior/API/data/dependency classification
typed independent preflight review passes before implementation
custom code requires every reuse candidate to be reused or rejected
positive and negative fixtures prove conditional gate enforcement
canonical and template skill trees are byte-identical
decision source events resolve through the hashed catalog
both reviews explicitly pass outcome, component-substitution, reuse, evidence, end-to-end, and cost-scale checks
clean-context reviewer names the v2.1.1 architecture, exclusions, commands, and next step without legacy reads
implementation review has no unresolved findings
source lock reports published with release v2.1.1 only after verification on main
tag v2.1.1 resolves to the verified release commit
main and tag v2.1.1 are pushed
